chunk_summary: Zinc and ORS are critical for managing diarrhea, reducing recurrent morbidity, and supporting linear growth in children, with WHO-recommended dosages and integrated WASH practices to prevent stunting.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of zinc and ORS in managing acute diarrhea and preventing stunting.
Learn WHO-recommended zinc dosages for children with acute diarrhea.
Identify key WASH practices to prevent recurrent diarrhea and undernutrition.
Recognize when to escalate care for severe or persistent diarrhea.
key_facts: Recurrent diarrhea contributes to stunting through nutrient loss, appetite reduction, and inflammation.
WHO recommends 20 mg/day of zinc for children ≥6 months and 10 mg/day for infants <6 months for 10–14 days during acute diarrhea.
Zinc reduces the duration and severity of diarrhea and lowers the risk of subsequent episodes.
ORS, continued feeding, and breastfeeding are essential during diarrhea to prevent undernutrition.
WASH practices, including safe water storage, handwashing, and proper feces disposal, are critical for prevention.
Escalate care for blood in stool, severe dehydration, persistent fever, or prolonged diarrhea with weight faltering.
reference_quote: The WHO recommends therapeutic zinc for 10–14 days with acute diarrhea: 20 mg/day for children 6 months and older and 10 mg/day for infants under 6 months. (Source: WHO, Reference 1)
document_key_topics: ORS and feeding practices during diarrhea
WASH interventions to prevent recurrent diarrhea
Escalation of care for severe or persistent diarrhea
